response.justification: Sarah Smith has extensive experience in software development across multiple languages and domains, including C and C++ (National Instruments), concurrent programming, and performance optimization. However, her recent experience leans heavily toward JavaScript/TypeScript, Node.js, and web/mobile development, with limited exposure to industrial automation, IoT protocols (e.g., Modbus, OPC UA), or IEC 62443 cybersecurity standards. Her C++ experience is not recent (2014-2015) and lacks depth in secure development lifecycle (SDL) practices or cloud-native solutions.
strengths: Strong C/C++ background with hands-on experience in low-latency applications and real-time systems (National Instruments).
Extensive full-stack development experience, demonstrating adaptability and problem-solving skills in diverse domains.
Active open-source contributions and mentorship (e.g., Refactoring Dojos, workshops, and community engagement).
concerns: Limited recent C++ experience (last significant C++ work was in 2014-2015) and no clear evidence of modern C++ (e.g., C++17/20) or STL proficiency.
No demonstrated experience with industrial automation, IoT protocols (Modbus, OPC UA), or IEC 62443 cybersecurity standards.
Cloud platform experience is not highlighted; recent work focuses on Node.js, React, and AWS CDK, which may not align with the role's cloud requirements (Azure).
